The dance features four young performers carrying large bamboo decorations called ‘Shinai’ and holding drums, dancing in a circle. 『白鳥踊り保存会五十年史』（1997th year ）42頁 The Shinai, originally a rain prayer tool, evolved into a ceremonial centerpiece, driving out drought demons and celebrating harvests. The word ‘Kake’ in ‘Kakeodori’ refers to ‘Gankake’ (願掛け), meaning prayer offerings. == Kanomizu no Kakeodori == The largest of its kind, the dance moved from Motai Village to Kanomizu Village in 1709 (宝永6th year ). While often mistaken as a festival of Shirakawa Shrine, it is part of the regional ceremony.『明宝寒水史』（2011th year ）102頁-108頁 In the Meiji era, it was temporarily banned due to the separation of Shinto and Buddhism but revived in 1887. Since 2010, it is held on the second Sunday of September and its preceding day. == References == * 『郡上八幡町史 下巻』(1961st year ) * 『明宝寒水史』(2011th year ) == Related Links == * [https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/170425 Kanomizu no Kakeodori on Bunkazai Online] * {{ill|Kansui Kake Odori|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kansui Kake Odori|qq=}} ; [[:en:Shizuoka Prefecture|Shizuoka Prefecture]] * {{ill|Tokuyama Bon Odori|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokuyama Bon Odori|qq=}} * {{ill|Utomaki Bon Odori|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Utomaki Bon Odori|qq=}} ; [[:en:Aichi Prefecture|Aichi Prefecture]] * {{ill|Ayato Night Nembutsu and Bon Odori|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ayato Night Nembutsu and Bon Odori|qq=}} ; [[:en:Mie Prefecture|Mie Prefecture]] * {{ill|Katte Shrine Ritual Dances|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katte Shrine Ritual Dances|qq=}} {{translated page|ja|寒水の掛踊|version=104359588|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 5]] }} [[ja:寒水の掛踊]] [[ja:徳山の盆踊]] [[ja:有東木の盆踊]] [[ja:綾渡の夜念仏と盆踊]] [[ja:勝手神社の神事踊]] [[Category:Festivals in Gifu Prefecture]] [[Category:Intangible Folk Cultural Properties that need measures such as documentation of Japan]] [[Category:Culture of Gujo City]] [[Category:Important Intangible Folk Cultural Properties]] [[Category:Furyū-odori, ritual dances imbued with people's hopes and prayers]] {{wikidata link|Q119303611}}

Since 2010, it is held on the second Sunday of September and its preceding day. == References == * 『郡上八幡町史 下巻』(1961st year ) * 『明宝寒水史』(2011th year ) == Related Links == * [https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/170425 Kanomizu no Kakeodori on Bunkazai Online] * {{ill|Kanmizu no Kake Odori|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanmizu no Kake Odori|qq=}} ; [[:en:Shizuoka Prefecture|Shizuoka Prefecture]] * {{ill|Tokuyama Bon Odori|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokuyama Bon Odori|qq=}} * {{ill|Utomaki Bon Odori|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Utomaki Bon Odori|qq=}} ; [[:en:Aichi Prefecture|Aichi Prefecture]] * {{ill|Ayato Night Nembutsu and Bon Odori|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ayato Night Nembutsu and Bon Odori|qq=}} ; [[:en:Mie Prefecture|Mie Prefecture]] * {{ill|Katte Shrine Ritual Dances|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katte Shrine Ritual Dances|qq=}} {{translated page|ja|寒水の掛踊|version=104359588|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 5]] }} [[ja:寒水の掛踊]] [[ja:徳山の盆踊]] [[ja:有東木の盆踊]] [[ja:綾渡の夜念仏と盆踊]] [[ja:勝手神社の神事踊]] [[Category:Festivals in Gifu Prefecture]] [[Category:Intangible Folk Cultural Properties that need measures such as documentation of Japan]] [[Category:Culture of Gujo City]] [[Category:Important Intangible Folk Cultural Properties]] [[Category:Furyū-odori, ritual dances imbued with people's hopes and prayers]] {{wikidata link|Q119303611}}

Also known as '''Kakeodori''' (掛け踊) or '''Kaki Odori''' (嘉喜踊り, 賀喜踊り).『郡上八幡町史 下巻』（1961st year ）888頁-893頁 == Overview == === About Gujo’s Kaki Odori === The Kakeodori is a type of {{ill|Furyū-odori|ja|風流踊り|1=Furyū-odori|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Furyū-odori|13=User:Immanuelle/Furyū-odori|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 風流踊}}. The word ‘Kake’ in ‘Kakeodori’ refers to ‘Gankake’ (願掛け), meaning prayer offerings. == Kanomizu no Kakeodori == The largest of its kind, the dance moved from Motai Village to Kanomizu Village in 1709 (宝永6th year ). {{ill|Shirakawa Shrine (Gujo)|ja|白山神社 (郡上市明宝寒水)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shirakawa Shrine (Gujo)|1=Hakusan Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q119929604}} | 主催 = 掛踊保存会 }} '''Kanomizu no Kakeodori''' (寒水の掛踊) is a taiko dance performed at {{ill|Shirakawa Shrine (Gujo)|ja|白山神社 (郡上市明宝寒水)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shirakawa Shrine (Gujo)|1=Hakusan Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q119929604}} in Meihou Kanomizu, Gujo, Gifu Prefecture. It is designated as a {{ill|Important Intangible Cultural Property of Japan|ja|重要無形文化財|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Important Intangible Cultural Property of Japan|1=Important Intangible Cultural Property of Japan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11644860}}. Also known as '''Kakeodori''' (掛け踊) or '''Kaki Odori''' (嘉喜踊り, 賀喜踊り).『郡上八幡町史 下巻』（1961st year ）888頁-893頁 == Overview == === About Gujo’s Kaki Odori === The Kakeodori is a type of {{ill|Furyū-odori|ja|風流踊り|1=Furyū-odori|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Furyū-odori|13=User:Immanuelle/Furyū-odori|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 風流踊}}. Variations are found across shrines in Gujo, such as Nakatsuya and Tsubosa, but only Kanomizu’s dance remains regularly performed. The dance features four young performers carrying large bamboo decorations called ‘Shinai’ and holding drums, dancing in a circle. 『白鳥踊り保存会五十年史』（1997th year ）42頁 The Shinai, originally a rain prayer tool, evolved into a ceremonial centerpiece, driving out drought demons and celebrating harvests. The word ‘Kake’ in ‘Kakeodori’ refers to ‘Gankake’ (願掛け), meaning prayer offerings. == Kanomizu no Kakeodori == The largest of its kind, the dance moved from Motai Village to Kanomizu Village in 1709 (宝永6th year ). While often mistaken as a festival of Shirakawa Shrine, it is part of the regional ceremony.『明宝寒水史』（2011th year ）102頁-108頁 In the Meiji era, it was temporarily banned due to the separation of Shinto and Buddhism but revived in 1887. Since 2010, it is held on the second Sunday of September and its preceding day. == References == * 『郡上八幡町史 下巻』(1961st year ) * 『明宝寒水史』(2011th year ) == Related Links == * [https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/170425 Kanomizu no Kakeodori on Bunkazai Online] * {{ill|Kanmizu no Kake Odori|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanmizu no Kake Odori|qq=}} ; [[:en:Shizuoka Prefecture|Shizuoka Prefecture]] * {{ill|Tokuyama Bon Odori|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokuyama Bon Odori|qq=}} * {{ill|Utomaki Bon Odori|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Utomaki Bon Odori|qq=}} ; [[:en:Aichi Prefecture|Aichi Prefecture]] * {{ill|Ayato Night Nembutsu and Bon Odori|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ayato Night Nembutsu and Bon Odori|qq=}} ; [[:en:Mie Prefecture|Mie Prefecture]] * {{ill|Katte Shrine Ritual Dances|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katte Shrine Ritual Dances|qq=}} {{translated page|ja|寒水の掛踊|version=104359588|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 5]] }} [[ja:寒水の掛踊]] [[ja:徳山の盆踊]] [[ja:有東木の盆踊]] [[ja:綾渡の夜念仏と盆踊]] [[ja:勝手神社の神事踊]] [[Category:Festivals in Gifu Prefecture]] [[Category:Intangible Folk Cultural Properties that need measures such as documentation of Japan]] [[Category:Culture of Gujo City]] [[Category:Important Intangible Folk Cultural Properties]] [[Category:Furyū-odori, ritual dances imbued with people's hopes and prayers]] {{wikidata link|Q119303611}} [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]]

[[ja:寒水の掛踊]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q119303611]] Wikidata name: Kanmizu no Kake Odori [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:23, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q119303611]] Wikidata name: Kanmizu no Kake Odori [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 04:03, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{参照方法|date=2022nd year 3月4日 (金) 14:07 (UTC)}} {{イベントインフォメーション | イベント名称 = 寒水の掛踊 | 英文表記 = Kanomizu no Kakeodori | 画像 = Kanomizu no Kakeodori, Gujo, 2018.jpg | 画像サイズ = 300px | 画像説明 = 2018th year 撮影、「拝殿前の踊り」が始まる直前の様子 | 種類 = {{ill|festival|ja|祭り|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/festival|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 祭|1=festival|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q132241}} | 開催時期 = 9月第2日曜日とその前日 | 会場 = 掛踊伝承館
{{ill|Shirakawa Shrine (Gujo)|ja|白山神社 (郡上市明宝寒水)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shirakawa Shrine (Gujo)|1=Hakusan Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q119929604}} | 主催 = 掛踊保存会 }} '''Kanomizu no Kakeodori''' (寒水の掛踊) is a taiko dance performed at {{ill|Shirakawa Shrine (Gujo)|ja|白山神社 (郡上市明宝寒水)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shirakawa Shrine (Gujo)|1=Hakusan Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q119929604}} in Meihou Kanomizu, Gujo, Gifu Prefecture. It is designated as a {{ill|Important Intangible Cultural Property of Japan|ja|重要無形文化財|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Important Intangible Cultural Property of Japan|1=Important Intangible Cultural Property of Japan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11644860}}. Also known as '''Kakeodori''' (掛け踊) or '''Kaki Odori''' (嘉喜踊り, 賀喜踊り).『郡上八幡町史 下巻』（1961st year ）888頁-893頁 == Overview == === About Gujo’s Kaki Odori === The Kakeodori is a type of {{ill|Furyū-odori|ja|風流踊り|1=Furyū-odori|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Furyū-odori|13=User:Immanuelle/Furyū-odori|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 風流踊}}. Variations are found across shrines in Gujo, such as Nakatsuya and Tsubosa, but only Kanomizu’s dance remains regularly performed. The dance features four young performers carrying large bamboo decorations called ‘Shinai’ and holding drums, dancing in a circle. 『白鳥踊り保存会五十年史』（1997th year ）42頁 The Shinai, originally a rain prayer tool, evolved into a ceremonial centerpiece, driving out drought demons and celebrating harvests. The word ‘Kake’ in ‘Kakeodori’ refers to ‘Gankake’ (願掛け), meaning prayer offerings. == Kanomizu no Kakeodori == The largest of its kind, the dance moved from Motai Village to Kanomizu Village in 1709 (宝永6th year ). While often mistaken as a festival of Shirakawa Shrine, it is part of the regional ceremony.『明宝寒水史』（2011th year ）102頁-108頁 In the Meiji era, it was temporarily banned due to the separation of Shinto and Buddhism but revived in 1887. Since 2010, it is held on the second Sunday of September and its preceding day. == References == * 『郡上八幡町史 下巻』(1961st year ) * 『明宝寒水史』(2011th year ) == Related Links == * [https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/170425 Kanomizu no Kakeodori on Bunkazai Online] * {{ill|Kanmizu no Kake Odori|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanmizu no Kake Odori|qq=}} ; [[:en:Shizuoka Prefecture|Shizuoka Prefecture]] * {{ill|Tokuyama Bon Odori|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokuyama Bon Odori|qq=}} * {{ill|Utomaki Bon Odori|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Utomaki Bon Odori|qq=}} ; [[:en:Aichi Prefecture|Aichi Prefecture]] * {{ill|Ayato Night Nembutsu and Bon Odori|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ayato Night Nembutsu and Bon Odori|qq=}} ; [[:en:Mie Prefecture|Mie Prefecture]] * {{ill|Katte Shrine Ritual Dances|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katte Shrine Ritual Dances|qq=}} {{translated page|ja|寒水の掛踊|version=104359588|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 5]] }} [[ja:寒水の掛踊]] [[ja:徳山の盆踊]] [[ja:有東木の盆踊]] [[ja:綾渡の夜念仏と盆踊]] [[ja:勝手神社の神事踊]] [[Category:Festivals in Gifu Prefecture]] [[Category:Intangible Folk Cultural Properties that need measures such as documentation of Japan]] [[Category:Culture of Gujo City]] [[Category:Important Intangible Folk Cultural Properties]] [[Category:Furyū-odori, ritual dances imbued with people's hopes and prayers]] {{wikidata link|Q119303611}} [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]] [[Category:qq]]{{wikidata link|Q1}}[[Category:qq]] [[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]] [[Category:qq]]{{wikidata link|Q1}}[[Category:qq]] [[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]] [[Category:qq]]{{wikidata link|Q1}}[[Category:qq]] [[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]] [[Category:qq]]{{wikidata link|Q1}}[[Category:qq]]

Also known as '''Kakeodori''' (掛け踊) or '''Kaki Odori''' (嘉喜踊り, 賀喜踊り).『郡上八幡町史 下巻』（1961st year ）888頁-893頁 == Overview == === About Gujo’s Kaki Odori === The Kakeodori is a type of {{ill|Furyū-odori|ja|風流踊り|1=Furyū-odori|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Furyū-odori|13=User:Immanuelle/Furyū-odori|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 風流踊}}. Variations are found across shrines in Gujo, such as Nakatsuya and Tsubosa, but only Kanomizu’s dance remains regularly performed. The dance features four young performers carrying large bamboo decorations called ‘Shinai’ and holding drums, dancing in a circle. 『白鳥踊り保存会五十年史』（1997th year ）42頁 The Shinai, originally a rain prayer tool, evolved into a ceremonial centerpiece, driving out drought demons and celebrating harvests. The word ‘Kake’ in ‘Kakeodori’ refers to ‘Gankake’ (願掛け), meaning prayer offerings. == Kanomizu no Kakeodori == The largest of its kind, the dance moved from Motai Village to Kanomizu Village in 1709 (宝永6th year ). While often mistaken as a festival of Shirakawa Shrine, it is part of the regional ceremony.『明宝寒水史』（2011th year ）102頁-108頁 In the Meiji era, it was temporarily banned due to the separation of Shinto and Buddhism but revived in 1887. Since 2010, it is held on the second Sunday of September and its preceding day. == References == * 『郡上八幡町史 下巻』(1961st year ) * 『明宝寒水史』(2011th year ) == Related Links == * [https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/170425 Kanomizu no Kakeodori on Bunkazai Online] * {{ill|Kanmizu no Kake Odori|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanmizu no Kake Odori|qq=}} ; [[:en:Shizuoka Prefecture|Shizuoka Prefecture]] * {{ill|Tokuyama Bon Odori|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokuyama Bon Odori|qq=}} * {{ill|Utomaki Bon Odori|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Utomaki Bon Odori|qq=}} ; [[:en:Aichi Prefecture|Aichi Prefecture]] * {{ill|Ayato Night Nembutsu and Bon Odori|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ayato Night Nembutsu and Bon Odori|qq=}} ; [[:en:Mie Prefecture|Mie Prefecture]] * {{ill|Katte Shrine Ritual Dances|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katte Shrine Ritual Dances|qq=}} {{translated page|ja|寒水の掛踊|version=104359588|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 5]] }} [[ja:寒水の掛踊]] [[ja:徳山の盆踊]] [[ja:有東木の盆踊]] [[ja:綾渡の夜念仏と盆踊]] [[ja:勝手神社の神事踊]] [[Category:Festivals in Gifu Prefecture]] [[Category:Intangible Folk Cultural Properties that need measures such as documentation of Japan]] [[Category:Culture of Gujo City]] [[Category:Important Intangible Folk Cultural Properties]] [[Category:Furyū-odori, ritual dances imbued with people's hopes and prayers]] {{wikidata link|Q119303611}} [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]]

{{ill|Shirakawa Shrine (Gujo)|ja|白山神社 (郡上市明宝寒水)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shirakawa Shrine (Gujo)|1=Hakusan Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q119929604}} | 主催 = 掛踊保存会 }} '''Kanomizu no Kakeodori''' (寒水の掛踊) is a taiko dance performed at {{ill|Shirakawa Shrine (Gujo)|ja|白山神社 (郡上市明宝寒水)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shirakawa Shrine (Gujo)|1=Hakusan Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q119929604}} in Meihou Kanomizu, Gujo, Gifu Prefecture. It is designated as a {{ill|Important Intangible Cultural Property of Japan|ja|重要無形文化財|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Important Intangible Cultural Property of Japan|1=Important Intangible Cultural Property of Japan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11644860}}. Also known as '''Kakeodori''' (掛け踊) or '''Kaki Odori''' (嘉喜踊り, 賀喜踊り).『郡上八幡町史 下巻』（1961st year ）888頁-893頁 == Overview == === About Gujo’s Kaki Odori === The Kakeodori is a type of {{ill|Furyū-odori|ja|風流踊り|1=Furyū-odori|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Furyū-odori|13=User:Immanuelle/Furyū-odori|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 風流踊}}. Variations are found across shrines in Gujo, such as Nakatsuya and Tsubosa, but only Kanomizu’s dance remains regularly performed. The dance features four young performers carrying large bamboo decorations called ‘Shinai’ and holding drums, dancing in a circle. 『白鳥踊り保存会五十年史』（1997th year ）42頁 The Shinai, originally a rain prayer tool, evolved into a ceremonial centerpiece, driving out drought demons and celebrating harvests. The word ‘Kake’ in ‘Kakeodori’ refers to ‘Gankake’ (願掛け), meaning prayer offerings. == Kanomizu no Kakeodori == The largest of its kind, the dance moved from Motai Village to Kanomizu Village in 1709 (宝永6th year ). While often mistaken as a festival of Shirakawa Shrine, it is part of the regional ceremony.『明宝寒水史』（2011th year ）102頁-108頁 In the Meiji era, it was temporarily banned due to the separation of Shinto and Buddhism but revived in 1887. Since 2010, it is held on the second Sunday of September and its preceding day. == References == * 『郡上八幡町史 下巻』(1961st year ) * 『明宝寒水史』(2011th year ) == Related Links == * [https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/170425 Kanomizu no Kakeodori on Bunkazai Online] * {{ill|Kanmizu no Kake Odori|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanmizu no Kake Odori|qq=}} ; [[:en:Shizuoka Prefecture|Shizuoka Prefecture]] * {{ill|Tokuyama Bon Odori|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokuyama Bon Odori|qq=}} * {{ill|Utomaki Bon Odori|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Utomaki Bon Odori|qq=}} ; [[:en:Aichi Prefecture|Aichi Prefecture]] * {{ill|Ayato Night Nembutsu and Bon Odori|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ayato Night Nembutsu and Bon Odori|qq=}} ; [[:en:Mie Prefecture|Mie Prefecture]] * {{ill|Katte Shrine Ritual Dances|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katte Shrine Ritual Dances|qq=}} {{translated page|ja|寒水の掛踊|version=104359588|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| }} [[ja:寒水の掛踊]] [[ja:徳山の盆踊]] [[ja:有東木の盆踊]] [[ja:綾渡の夜念仏と盆踊]] [[ja:勝手神社の神事踊]] {{wikidata link|Q119303611}} [[Category:Completion tier 5]] [[Category:Culture of Gujo City]] [[Category:Festivals in Gifu Prefecture]] [[Category:Furyū-odori, ritual dances imbued with people's hopes and prayers]] [[Category:Important Intangible Folk Cultural Properties]] [[Category:Intangible Folk Cultural Properties that need measures such as documentation of Japan]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]]

[[ja:寒水の掛踊]] [[ja:寒水の掛踊]] [[ja:寒水の掛踊]] [[ja:寒水の掛踊]] {{参照方法|date=2022nd year 3月4日 (金) 14:07 (UTC)}} {{イベントインフォメーション | イベント名称 = 寒水の掛踊 | 英文表記 = Kanomizu no Kakeodori | 画像 = Kanomizu no Kakeodori, Gujo, 2018.jpg | 画像サイズ = 300px | 画像説明 = 2018th year 撮影、「拝殿前の踊り」が始まる直前の様子 | 種類 = {{ill|festival|ja|祭り|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/festival|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 祭|1=festival|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q132241}} | 開催時期 = 9月第2日曜日とその前日 | 会場 = 掛踊伝承館
{{ill|Shirakawa Shrine (Gujo)|ja|白山神社 (郡上市明宝寒水)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shirakawa Shrine (Gujo)|1=Hakusan Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q119929604}} | 主催 = 掛踊保存会 }} '''Kanomizu no Kakeodori''' (寒水の掛踊) is a taiko dance performed at {{ill|Shirakawa Shrine (Gujo)|ja|白山神社 (郡上市明宝寒水)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shirakawa Shrine (Gujo)|1=Hakusan Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q119929604}} in Meihou Kanomizu, Gujo, Gifu Prefecture. It is designated as a {{ill|Important Intangible Cultural Property of Japan|ja|重要無形文化財|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Important Intangible Cultural Property of Japan|1=Important Intangible Cultural Property of Japan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11644860}}. Also known as '''Kakeodori''' (掛け踊) or '''Kaki Odori''' (嘉喜踊り, 賀喜踊り).『郡上八幡町史 下巻』（1961st year ）888頁-893頁 == Overview == === About Gujo’s Kaki Odori === The Kakeodori is a type of {{ill|Furyū-odori|ja|風流踊り|1=Furyū-odori|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Furyū-odori|13=User:Immanuelle/Furyū-odori|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 風流踊}}. Variations are found across shrines in Gujo, such as Nakatsuya and Tsubosa, but only Kanomizu’s dance remains regularly performed. The dance features four young performers carrying large bamboo decorations called ‘Shinai’ and holding drums, dancing in a circle. 『白鳥踊り保存会五十年史』（1997th year ）42頁 The Shinai, originally a rain prayer tool, evolved into a ceremonial centerpiece, driving out drought demons and celebrating harvests. The word ‘Kake’ in ‘Kakeodori’ refers to ‘Gankake’ (願掛け), meaning prayer offerings. == Kanomizu no Kakeodori == The largest of its kind, the dance moved from Motai Village to Kanomizu Village in 1709 (宝永6th year ). While often mistaken as a festival of Shirakawa Shrine, it is part of the regional ceremony.『明宝寒水史』（2011th year ）102頁-108頁 In the Meiji era, it was temporarily banned due to the separation of Shinto and Buddhism but revived in 1887. Since 2010, it is held on the second Sunday of September and its preceding day. == References == * 『郡上八幡町史 下巻』(1961st year ) * 『明宝寒水史』(2011th year ) == Related Links == * [https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/170425 Kanomizu no Kakeodori on Bunkazai Online] * {{ill|Kanmizu no Kake Odori|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanmizu no Kake Odori|qq=}} ; {{ill|Shizuoka Prefecture|en|Shizuoka Prefecture|WD=Q131320|lt=Shizuoka Prefecture}} * {{ill|Tokuyama Bon Odori|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokuyama Bon Odori|qq=}} * {{ill|Utomaki Bon Odori|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Utomaki Bon Odori|qq=}} ; {{ill|Aichi Prefecture|en|Aichi Prefecture|WD=Q80434|lt=Aichi Prefecture}} * {{ill|Ayato Night Nembutsu and Bon Odori|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ayato Night Nembutsu and Bon Odori|qq=}} ; {{ill|Mie Prefecture|en|Mie Prefecture|WD=Q128196|lt=Mie Prefecture}} * {{ill|Katte Shrine Ritual Dances|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katte Shrine Ritual Dances|qq=}} {{translated page|ja|寒水の掛踊|version=104359588|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:寒水の掛踊]] [[ja:徳山の盆踊]] [[ja:有東木の盆踊]] [[ja:綾渡の夜念仏と盆踊]] [[ja:勝手神社の神事踊]] {{wikidata link|Q119303611}} [[Category:Completion tier 5]] [[Category:Culture of Gujo City]] [[Category:Festivals in Gifu Prefecture]] [[Category:Furyū-odori, ritual dances imbued with people's hopes and prayers]] [[Category:Important Intangible Folk Cultural Properties]] [[Category:Intangible Folk Cultural Properties that need measures such as documentation of Japan]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]]

It is designated as a {{ill|Important Intangible Cultural Property of Japan|ja|重要無形文化財|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Important Intangible Cultural Property of Japan|1=Important Intangible Cultural Properties of Japan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11644860}}. Also known as '''Kakeodori''' (掛け踊) or '''Kaki Odori''' (嘉喜踊り, 賀喜踊り).『郡上八幡町史 下巻』（1961st year ）888頁-893頁 == Overview == === About Gujo’s Kaki Odori === The Kakeodori is a type of {{ill|Furyū-odori|ja|風流踊り|1=Furyū-odori|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Furyū-odori|13=User:Immanuelle/Furyū-odori|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 風流踊}}. Variations are found across shrines in Gujo, such as Nakatsuya and Tsubosa, but only Kanomizu’s dance remains regularly performed. The dance features four young performers carrying large bamboo decorations called ‘Shinai’ and holding drums, dancing in a circle. 『白鳥踊り保存会五十年史』（1997th year ）42頁 The Shinai, originally a rain prayer tool, evolved into a ceremonial centerpiece, driving out drought demons and celebrating harvests. The word ‘Kake’ in ‘Kakeodori’ refers to ‘Gankake’ (願掛け), meaning prayer offerings. == Kanomizu no Kakeodori == The largest of its kind, the dance moved from Motai Village to Kanomizu Village in 1709 (宝永6th year ). While often mistaken as a festival of Shirakawa Shrine, it is part of the regional ceremony.『明宝寒水史』（2011th year ）102頁-108頁 In the Meiji era, it was temporarily banned due to the separation of Shinto and Buddhism but revived in 1887. Since 2010, it is held on the second Sunday of September and its preceding day. == References == * 『郡上八幡町史 下巻』(1961st year ) * 『明宝寒水史』(2011th year ) == Related Links == * [https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/170425 Kanomizu no Kakeodori on Bunkazai Online] * {{ill|Kanmizu no Kake Odori|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanmizu no Kake Odori|qq=}} ; {{ill|Shizuoka Prefecture|en|Shizuoka Prefecture|WD=Q131320|lt=Shizuoka Prefecture|1=Shizuoka Prefecture}} * {{ill|Tokuyama Bon Odori|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokuyama Bon Odori|qq=}} * {{ill|Utomaki Bon Odori|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Utomaki Bon Odori|qq=}} ; {{ill|Aichi Prefecture|en|Aichi Prefecture|WD=Q80434|lt=Aichi Prefecture|1=Aichi Prefecture}} * {{ill|Ayato Night Nembutsu and Bon Odori|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ayato Night Nembutsu and Bon Odori|qq=}} ; {{ill|Mie Prefecture|en|Mie Prefecture|WD=Q128196|lt=Mie Prefecture|1=Mie Prefecture}} * {{ill|Katte Shrine Ritual Dances|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katte Shrine Ritual Dances|qq=}} {{translated page|ja|寒水の掛踊|version=104359588|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:寒水の掛踊]] [[ja:徳山の盆踊]] [[ja:有東木の盆踊]] [[ja:綾渡の夜念仏と盆踊]] [[ja:勝手神社の神事踊]] {{wikidata link|Q119303611}} [[Category:Culture of Gujo City]] [[Category:Festivals in Gifu Prefecture]] [[Category:Furyū-odori, ritual dances imbued with people's hopes and prayers]] [[Category:Important Intangible Folk Cultural Properties]] [[Category:Intangible Folk Cultural Properties that need measures such as documentation of Japan]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:pages]]
